title: Inverted index
body: An inverted index maps each term to the docs containing it. It is the central data structure of every full text search engine.

title: BM25 ranking
body: BM25 scores documents using term frequency and document length. It is the default ranker in most full text search engines.

title: Tokenization
body: A tokenizer splits text into tokens. Lowercasing and punctuation stripping happen before the inverted index is built.

title: Posting lists
body: Posting lists store document ids and term frequencies. They are encoded with variable byte or simd compression for fast scans.

title: Query parser
body: A query parser turns a user string into an abstract syntax tree of terms, conjunctions and disjunctions for the search engine to execute.

title: Learning to rank
body: Learning to rank reorders BM25 candidates using a machine learned model trained on relevance judgments. CatBoost is a popular choice.

title: Boolean queries
body: Boolean queries combine terms with AND and OR. The query parser maps these to conjunction and disjunction operators over the inverted index.

title: TF IDF intuition
body: Term frequency captures how often a token appears in a document. Inverse document frequency captures how rare the token is across the corpus.

title: Phrase queries
body: Phrase queries match documents where terms appear adjacent. They require positional postings, which the inverted index optionally stores.

title: Index segments
body: Search engines write data in immutable segments. Background compaction merges small segments into bigger ones to keep query latency low.

title: Sourdough bread
body: A simple sourdough recipe needs flour, water, salt and a wild yeast starter. Bake it in a dutch oven for a crispy crust.

title: Pasta carbonara
body: Carbonara uses guanciale, egg yolks, pecorino and black pepper. No cream. Toss the hot pasta off the heat to keep the eggs silky.

title: Knife skills
body: Sharp knives are safer than dull ones. Learn the rock chop, the julienne and the brunoise to speed up every recipe in your kitchen.

title: Sear a steak
body: Dry the steak, salt heavily, and sear in a hot cast iron pan with neutral oil. Rest the meat before slicing across the grain.

title: Vegetable stock
body: Vegetable stock simmers onion, carrot, celery and herbs in water. Strain after an hour and freeze in jars for soups and risottos.

title: Caramel sauce
body: Cook sugar with a splash of water until amber, off the heat add cream and butter. The caramel sauce keeps in the fridge for two weeks.

title: Fresh pesto
body: Pesto blends basil leaves, garlic, pine nuts, parmesan and olive oil. Toss with hot pasta or spoon onto grilled bread for a quick lunch.

title: Roast chicken
body: Pat the chicken dry, salt under the skin, and roast at high heat. Rest before carving so the juices redistribute through the meat.

title: Risotto
body: Risotto is rice cooked slowly with hot stock, finished with butter and parmesan. Constant stirring releases starch and creates a creamy sauce.

title: Chocolate chip cookies
body: Cream butter and sugar, beat in eggs, fold in flour and chocolate. Chill the dough for a deeper flavor, then bake until the edges crisp.

title: Marathon training
body: A marathon plan ramps weekly mileage gradually, peaks three weeks before race day and tapers to keep the legs fresh for twenty six miles.

title: Soccer offside
body: A player is offside when, at the moment a teammate plays the ball, they are nearer the goal line than the second to last opponent.

title: Basketball pick and roll
body: The pick and roll is a two player action where one sets a screen and rolls to the basket while the other drives off the screen.

title: Tennis serve
body: A good tennis serve starts with a relaxed toss, a full kinetic chain through the legs and core, and a snap of the wrist at contact.

title: Olympic weightlifting
body: The snatch and the clean and jerk are the two Olympic lifts. Both move a barbell from the floor to overhead in one or two phases.

title: Cycling cadence
body: Cadence is pedal revolutions per minute. Most cyclists train at eighty to ninety rpm on flats and lower cadence on steep climbs.

title: Swimming freestyle
body: A balanced freestyle stroke rotates the hips, breathes to alternating sides, and finishes each pull with the hand exiting at the thigh.

title: Rock climbing grades
body: Sport climbing routes are graded by difficulty. The yosemite decimal system runs from five point zero up to five point fifteen and beyond.

title: Boxing footwork
body: Boxing footwork keeps the feet shoulder width apart, weight forward, and small steps that maintain balance through punches and counters.

title: Yoga sun salutation
body: The sun salutation flows from mountain pose through forward folds, planks and downward dogs. It warms the body before a longer yoga session.
